clean_code:
from zipline.api import order_target_percent, schedule_function, date_rules, time_rules, get_datetime
from zipline import run_algorithm
import pandas as pd

def initialize(context):
    context.invested = False
    schedule_function(rebalance, date_rules.every_day(), time_rules.market_open())

def rebalance(context, data):
    current_date = get_datetime()
    end_date = pd.Timestamp('2023-12-31', tz='UTC')

    # Exit all positions on last day
    if current_date >= end_date:
        order_target_percent(context.asset, 0.0)
        return

    if context.signal > 0 and not context.invested:
        order_target_percent(context.asset, 1.0)
        context.invested = True
    elif context.signal <= 0 and context.invested:
        order_target_percent(context.asset, 0.0)
        context.invested = False
description: Strategy enters positions but never has exit logic to close them.
explanation: Without exit conditions, a position taken early in the backtest holds through the entire remaining period regardless of market conditions. This is usually not intentional — the strategy's intent was to trade signals but the exit logic was forgotten. The reported P&L includes the full mark-to-market of a permanently held position.
suggested_fix: Always implement explicit exit conditions and ensure positions are closed at end of backtest.

issues.title: Strategy has no exit logic — positions held indefinitely
